externalDocs.description: Find out more about Netatmo API

get.summary: Retrieve user's homes and their topology.
get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the actual topology and static information of all devices present into a user account.
It is possible to specify a home_id to focus on one home or to specify a gateway_type to focus on one type of gateway.


Scope to use:
  • read_magellan for Legrand Wiring device or Electrical panel products
  • read_smarther for Smarther with Netatmo thermostat
  • read_thermostat for Smart Modulating thermostat
  • read_bubendorff for Bubbendorf shutters
  • read_mhs1 for the MyHome Server 1 modules

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Filter by Home ID

parameters.name: gateway_types
parameters.description: Filter by Gateway Type {BNS, NLG, OTH, NBG, BNMH}

pending_user_consent.description: Whether the legal consent is accepted. This relates to the country of the user

get.summary: Get the current status of a home and the associated devices
get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the actual status of all devices present into a specific home.
It is possible to specify a list of gateways using device_types to filter the returned devices.

Scope to use:
  • read_magellan for Legrand Wiring device or Electrical panel products
  • read_smarther for Smarther with Netatmo thermostat
  • read_thermostat for Smart Modulating thermostat
  • read_bubendorff for Bubbendorf shutters
  • read_mhs1 for the MyHome Server 1 modules

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: device_types
parameters.description: Filter by Gateway Types {BNS, NLG, OTH, NBG, BNMH}

post.description:
This endpoint permits to modify the actual status of devices present into a specific home.
Parameters are sent through the request body using a json format. The json contains the same format as the body of the answer of a homestatus method (see the different editable examples).
For heating modules, the state can be controlled at the room level.
For other modules, the state is controlled at the module level.

Scope to use:
  • write_magellan for Legrand Wiring device or Electrical panel products
  • write_smarther for Smarther with Netatmo thermostat
  • write_thermostat for Smart Modulating thermostat
  • write_bubendorff for Bubbendorf shutters

Turn on the module.summary: Turn on the module (NLP, NLPM, NLC, BNCS)

Turn on the light.summary: Turn on the light (NLF, NLM, NLL, NLPT, BNIL)

Set a setpoint of the room to Eco mode (.summary: Set a setpoint of the room to Eco mode (Controlled by NLC)

Set the room to frost guard mode (Contro.summary: Set the room to frost guard mode (Controlled by NLC)

Set the room to comfort mode (Controlled.summary: Set the room to comfort mode (Controlled by NLC)

Apply a temperature setpoint to a room (.summary: Apply a temperature setpoint to a room (BNS, BNTH)

Set a room to BOOST (for Smarther in hea.summary: Set a room to BOOST (for Smarther in heating). It can only be applied for 30, 60 or 90 minutes

Change a room mode to frostguard (BNS, B.summary: Change a room mode to frostguard (for Smarther in heating)

Let a room follow the home schedule (BNS.summary: Let a room follow the home schedule (for Smarther in heating)

Apply a temperature setpoint to a room (.summary: Apply a temperature setpoint to a room (Controlled by Smarther set to Heating). The setpoint end time is optional

Set a room to BOOST (Controlled by Smart.summary: Set a room to BOOST (Controlled by Smarther in Heating mode). It can only be applied for 30, 60 or 90 minutes

Change a room mode to off (for Smarther.summary: Change a room mode to frostguard (for Smarther in cooling)

Let a room follow the home schedule (for.summary: Let a room follow the home schedule (for Smarther in cooling)

Modify states of many modules.summary: Modify states of many modules at once

get.summary: Retrieve scenarios of devices associated to the home
get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the list of scenarios associated to a home. 2 types of information are retrieved:
  • The list of scenarios
  • The list of modules and their behavior associated to each scenario


Scope to use:
  • read_magellan for Legrand Wiring device or Electrical panel products
  • read_bubendorff for Bubbendorf shutters
  • read_mhs1 for the MyHome Server 1 modules

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

get.summary: Retrieve data history of a room (eg to display graphs)
get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the history of data associated to a room.

Scope to use:
  • read_smarther for Smarther with Netatmo thermostat
  • read_thermostat for Smart Modulating thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: room_id
parameters.description: Room ID

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Timestamp of the last measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type of data to be returned {temperature, sp_temperature, min_temp, max_temp, date_min_temp, date_max_temp}.
Date data are only available for scales from 1 day to 1 month and unit is Local Unix Time in seconds.

parameters.name: date_begin
parameters.description:
Timestamp of the first measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds).
By default, it will retrieve the oldest data available.

parameters.name: date_end
parameters.description: Timestamp of the last measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of measurements (default and max are 1024)

post.description:
This endpoint permits to control the heating of a specific home. A home can be set in 3 differents modes:
  • "schedule" mode in which the home will follow the user schedule
  • "away" mode which will put the whole house to away (default is 12° but can be changed by the user in its settings)
  • "hg" corresponds to frostguard mode (7° by default)


Scope to use:
  • write_smarther for Smarther with Netatmo thermostat
  • write_thermostat for Smart Modulating thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Set the home heating system to use {schedule, away, hg}

parameters.name: endtime
parameters.description: Enddate of the therm mode (Local Unix Time in seconds). It's only settable  for "hg" and "away" mode

get.description:
This endpoint permits to get historical measure for devices.

Scope to use:
  • read_magellan for Legrand Wiring device or Electrical panel products (consumption data)
  • read_smarther for Smarther with Netatmo thermostat (boiler/cooler status)
  • read_thermostat for Smart Modulating thermostat (boiler status)

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Gateway Mac Address

parameters.name: module_id
parameters.description: Module Mac Adress

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Timeframe between two measurements {30min, 1hour, 3hours, 1day, 1week, 1month}

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type of data to be returned {boileron, boileroff, sum_boiler_on, sum_boiler_off, sum_energy_elec, sum_energy_elec$0, sum_energy_elec$1, sum_energy_elec$2, sum_energy_price}
Boileron/off is only available for scales from 30 to 3hours and sums of boiler on/off for scales from 1day to 1month.
  • boileron = Average number of minutes the boiler is active per hour
  • boileroff = Average number of minutes the boiler is inactive per hour
  • sum_boiler_on = Sum of minutes the boiler is active during the scale time period
  • sum_boiler_off = Sum of minutes the boiler is inactive during the scale time period
  • sum_energy_elec = Cumulated energy consumption during the scale time period in case there is no electricity contract
  • sum_energy_elec$0/1/2 = Cumulated energy consumption during the scale time period in case there is an electricity contract:
    • $0 in case of a basic tariff (always the same tariff )
    • $1 for peak time periods
    • $2 for off peak time periods
  • sum_energy_price = Cumulated energy price during the scale time periods in case there is no electricity contract
  • sum_energy_price$0/1/2 = Cumulated energy price during the scale time periods in case there is an electricity contract:
    • $0 in case of a basic tariff (always the same tariff )
    • $1 for peak time periods
    • $2 for off peak time periods

parameters.name: date_begin
parameters.description:
Timestamp of the first measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds).
By default, it will retrieve the oldest data available.

parameters.name: date_end
parameters.description: Timestamp of the last measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds)

post.description:
This endpoint permits to create a new schedule of a home.
Several types of schedules can be created
  • Heating schedule
  • Cooling schedule
  • Event schedule
  • Electricity schedule
The parameters specific to a type of planning and the planning definition composed of a timetable array and a zone array are sent via the body.

Scope to use:
write_smarther, write_magellan

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Home ID

post.description:
This endpoint permits to edit an existing schedule.
It is possible to only rename the schedule or to edit the timetable and/or the zones.
If no schedule_id is specified, then the current active schedule is modified. Schedules parameters are similar to /createnewhomeschedule endpoint.

Scope to use:
write_smarther, write_magellan

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: Id of the schedule. If not defined, the current schedule is modified

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the schedule

Update the name of a heating schedule.summary: Update the name of a schedule

post.description:
This endpoint permits to apply a specific schedule to a home

Scope to use:
write_smarther, write_magellan

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: Id of the schedule to switch on

therm_relay.description:
the main device id of the heating module that controls this room

For example, in Home + Control product, the cable outlet with "radiator" appliance type is considered as a heating module
which can control the temperature or heating mode of the room. This module is necessary to be assigned to a room
then, the therm_relay of the room will be the main device id of that radiator

Module.description:
Module represents a IoT product of Netatmo ecosystem. Module can classify into two category:
- "main device" is a device that can function properly with or without other modules
- "module" is a device that can function only if it associates with the "main device"

type.description:
module type
For Home + Control products:
  * `NLG` - Gateway
  * `NLP` - Socket
  * `NLF` - Switch
  * `NLT` - Remote
  * `NLM` - Micromodule
  * `NLV` - Roller shutter
  * `NLL` - Italian switch
  * `NLPC` - Energy meter
  * `NLPM` - Mobile socket
  * `NLPO` - Connected contactor
  * `NLC` - Cable outlet
  * `BNS` - Smarther with Netatmo
  * `BNMH` - MyHome Server 1
  * `BNTH` - Bticino Thermostat
  * `BNFC` - Bticino Fan coil
  * `BNTR` - Bticino module towel rail
  * `BNCS` - Bticino module Controlled Socket
  * `BNXM` - Bticino X meter
  * `BNLD` - Bticino module lighting dimmer
  * `BNIL` - Bticino intelligent light
  * `BN3L` - Bticino 3rd party light
  * `BNAB` - Bticino module automatic blind
  * `BNAS` - Bticino module automatic shutter
  * `BNMS` - Bticino module motorized shade

setup_date.description: the last installation date in unix timestamp

appliance_type.description: appliance type of the module. This is only for Home + Control products

room_id.description: the room id where this module is installed

bridge.description:
the main device id to which this module associates
if this module itself is a main device such as Legrand Gateway, this field will be absent

type.description: type of the zone (0 = day, 1 = night, 2 = away, 3 = frost guard, 4 = custom, 5 = eco, 8 = comfort)
type of the zone (0 = day, 1 = night, 2 = away, 3 = frost guard, 4 = custom, 5 = eco, 8 = comfort)

therm_setpoint_fp.description: Heating setpoint mode for a room controlled by a pilot wire

selected.description: true if the schedule is selected

type.description: type of schedule (can be either event or electrical for Legrand product)

target_position.description: Position of the roller shutter. The steps depend on the target_position:step of the device

tariff_option.description: Tariff option (basic = always the same price, peak_and_off_peak = peak & off peak hours)

selected.description: true if the schedule is selected

type.description: type of schedule (can be either event or electrical for Legrand product)
type of the zone (0 = day, 1 = night, 2 = away, 3 = frost guard, 4 = custom, 5 = eco, 8 = comfort)

price_type.description: Type of tariff during that zone

price_value.description: Price of electricity during that zone

twilight_offset.description: Offset in minutes from sunrise/sunset when the action is triggered

rooms.description:
statuses of rooms. Room being controlled by heating module will return temperature status such as therm_setpoint_end_time,
therm_setpoint_fp and therm_setpoint_mode. In order to know whether the room is controlled by heating module,
"therm_relay" would be returned in a room topology, see /api/homesdata

anticipating.description: whether a device is currently anticipating or not

open_window.description: indicates if a window is opened

heating_power_request.description: Represents the percentage of heating the valves of a room are asking for (valves only)

therm_setpoint_start_time.description: Start unix timestamp when a manual setpoint is set

therm_setpoint_end_time.description: The end time of a manual setpoint in unix timestamp. 2147483647 = until a new order

therm_setpoint_mode.description: The thermostat mode in which the room is. For a room controlled by a BNS, mode can be home, manual, max or hg/off (if heating/cooling). For a room controlled by a NLC, mode can be off, manual or hg.

cooling_setpoint_start_time.description: Start unix timestamp when a manual setpoint is set

cooling_setpoint_end_time.description: The end time of a manual setpoint in unix timestamp. 2147483647 = until a new order

cooling_setpoint_mode.description: The thermostat mode in which the room is. Off mode is used when the home is in cooling, hg (frostguard) is used when the home is in Heating

therm_setpoint_fp.description: Usually used to control 'Fil pilote (FP)' setpoint

ModuleStatus.description:
Statuses of device/module. The status field depends on the product type.
If the product doesn't support the feature, the field will be absent.

For example,
* NLP (Socket) has a feature to turn on/off, so the status field "on" will be presented. On the other hand,
* NLV (roller shutter) doesn't have this feature, so the status field "on" will be absent

type.description:
module type
For Home + Control products:
  * `NLP` - Socket
  * `NLF` - Switch
  * `NLT` - Remote
  * `NLM` - Micromodule
  * `NLV` - Roller shutter
  * `NLL` - Italian switch
  * `NLPC` - Energy meter
  * `NLPM` - Mobile socket
  * `NLPO` - Connected contactor
  * `NLC` - Cable outlet
  * `BNS` - Smarther with Netatmo
  * `OTH` - Smart Modulating Thermostat Relay
  * `OTM` - Smart Modulating Thermostat
  * `NRV` - Smart Valves
  * `BNMH` - MyHome Server 1
  * `BNTH` - Bticino Thermostat
  * `BNFC` - Bticino Fan coil
  * `BNTR` - Bticino module towel rail
  * `BNCS` - Bticino module Controlled Socket
  * `BNXM` - Bticino X meter
  * `BNLD` - Bticino module lighting dimmer
  * `BNIL` - Bticino intelligent light
  * `BN3L` - Bticino 3rd party light
  * `BNAB` - Bticino module automatic blind
  * `BNAS` - Bticino module automatic shutter
  * `BNMS` - Bticino module motorized shade

on.description: ON/OFF state of a switchable module

contactor_mode.description: The current mode of the contactor

boiler_status.description: Whether the boiler is ON or OFF

boiler_control.description: how the product is able to communicate with the boiler (e.g. on/off or opentherm) for OTH

dhw_control.description: whether the device is linked to a boiler controlling domestic hot water (used for OTH)

cooler_status.description: Whether the cooler is ON or OFF

current_position.description: Current position of a roller shutter

target_position.description: Target position of a roller shutter. 100 = open, 0 = close, -1 stop

target_position:step.description: Minimum step value of target_position. The target_position can only be incremented in multiples of this step value

brightness.description: Brightness level of a dimmable switch/light

battery_state.description: Battery state of a module. This is an interpretation of battery_level

last_seen.description: last unix timestamp that Gateway communicates with this module

power.description: a measure of the electric power being delivered by the module, in Watt.

offload.description: Whether or not the device has overcome its load. Only for NLP, NLPM, NLPO or NLC

offload_meters.description: List of smart shedder id used by offload algorithm

bridge.description:
the main device id to which this module associates
if this module itself is a main device such as Legrand Gateway, this field will be absent

customizable.description: whether the scenario can be customized by the user

Scenario.description:
A scenario that will apply command to modules when it's launched.
The available commands are the same one in setState, such as "on" for NLP

on.description: ON/OFF state that will apply on the module when the scenario is launched

target_position.description: Target position of a roller shutter that will apply on the module when the scenario is launched

SyncError.description: Error from physical devices such as the device can't parse the parameter

id.description: device/module id that causes the error

code.description:
error code
* 1 - Unknown error
* 2 - Internal error
* 3 - Parser error
* 4 - Command unknown node module error
* 5 - Command invalid params
* 6 - Unreachable

ApiError.description: Error at API level such as token is invalid

code.description:
API error code
* 1 - Missing access token
* 2 - Invalid access token
* 3 - Access token expired
* 4 - Inconsistency error
* 7 - Nothing to modify
* 9 - Device not found
* 10 - Missing parameters
* 11 - Internal error
* 13 - Forbidden operation
* 21 - Invalid parameters
* 23 - User not found
* 31 - Not found
